problem:
core: AIMessageChunk validator escapes non-ASCII in tool_call_chunks[].args. - I construct an `AIMessageChunk` from a `tool_calls` list (e.g. after deserializing a checkpoint), then read its `tool_call_chunks`.
- I expect each `tool_call_chunks[i]["args"]` string to round-trip the original tool args verbatim.
- Instead, non-ASCII characters in args (CJK, emoji, accents) come out escaped as `\uXXXX`. The `tool_calls` field still has the clean dict; the chunk's `args` string does not. Same message (npm config set registry is not working — it seems correct per old docs)
memory_hint: Use npmrc for registry config. The old syntax was removed.
failure_without_memory: Keeps using npm config set registry which is deprecated. Endless debugging.
